result:
Nirvana's second most selling studio album, "In Utero," was released on September 13, 1993, in the United Kingdom, and on September 21, 1993, for its full domestic release in the United States.[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/In_Utero), [2](https://www.last.fm/music/Nirvana/In+Utero/+wiki), [3](https://floodmagazine.com/148531/nirvana-in-utero-30th-anniversary-essay/)

"Nevermind" is Nirvana's best-selling album, having sold over 30 million copies globally.[4](https://www.90splease.com/in-utero-by-nirvana), [2](https://www.last.fm/music/Nirvana/In+Utero/+wiki) "In Utero," their third and final studio album, followed with approximately 7.8 million sales.[4](https://www.90splease.com/in-utero-by-nirvana), [2](https://www.last.fm/music/Nirvana/In+Utero/+wiki) Other significant releases include "MTV Unplugged in New York," a live album, with around 12.7 million sales, and their debut studio album "Bleach" with over 2.3 million sales.[4](https://www.90splease.com/in-utero-by-nirvana), [2](https://www.last.fm/music/Nirvana/In+Utero/+wiki)

direct_output:
**Process Summary:**
1. The query asked for the release date of Nirvana's second most selling studio album.
2. Initial steps involved searching Wikipedia and other sources to identify the albums and their release dates. The relevant pages included "Bleach (Nirvana album)", "Nirvana (band)", "Nevermind", "In Utero", and "Nirvana discography".
3. The Ground_Google_Search_Tool was used to find the specific release date of Nirvana's second most selling studio album, "In Utero".
4. The search results indicated that "In Utero" was released on September 13, 1993, in the United Kingdom, and on September 21, 1993, in the United States.

**Answer:**
<answer>September 21, 1993</answer>
